# Introduction

## Background

<!-- 
Provide context on:
- The growing prevalence of dementia and Alzheimer's disease
- Why early detection matters (treatment window, planning, clinical trials)
- The role of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) as a transitional stage
- Brief intro to the NACC database and its significance
-->

## Research Objectives

<!--
Clearly state what this project aims to achieve:
- Identify distinguishing features between stable and declining cognitive groups
- Evaluate predictive models for cognitive trajectory classification
- Explore whether disease regression (MCI → NL) has identifiable patterns
-->

## Scope and Limitations

<!--
- This is a preparatory research project, not a full clinical study
- Limitations of retrospective observational data
- Constraints of the available biomarker sample size (~384 patients)
-->
